title: Operational how-to
summary: Setting up a node, choosing a signer, receiving payments without a custodian, backup procedures, fee estimation, and what to do when something goes wrong. Concept-level explanations, not vendor recommendations.

edges.label: Running your own full node
edges.summary: What a full node actually does for you, what it doesn't, and the realistic resource and operational requirements as of 2026.

edges.label: Choosing a hardware signer
edges.summary: The properties to evaluate — open firmware, verifying display, vulnerability disclosure history — without the project recommending a specific vendor.
searchTerms: what to look for in a signer

edges.label: Receiving payments without a custodian
edges.summary: From a one-off donation to a recurring business inflow: how to accept Bitcoin directly to keys you control, on-chain or via Lightning.

edges.label: Backup procedures
edges.summary: What you actually need to back up (it's not just the seed), where to store it, how to test recovery, and the common failure modes.

edges.label: Fee estimation and getting a transaction confirmed
edges.summary: How fee markets work block to block, what the wallet's fee picker is reading, and what to do when an under-fee transaction is stuck.

edges.label: Recovery when things go wrong
edges.summary: Lost device with the seed safe; lost seed with the device safe; partial multisig key loss; sent to the wrong address. What's recoverable, what isn't, and the honest limit on what an outside expert can do.
